text.title: Feedback Loop Customer Reply Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Feedback Loop Customer Reply Templates application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: All replies MUST come from exactly five templates: acknowledge, build, waitlist, decline, bug. Other shapes route to manual review, not custom replies.
rule.rationale: Template-discipline research — anchors Feedback Loop Customer Reply Templates rule `five-templates-only`.

rule.statement: Every ticket MUST receive a reply within 72h of creation. Beyond 72h = customer assumes the channel is dead.
rule.rationale: Customer-feedback research — anchors Feedback Loop Customer Reply Templates rule `reply-within-72h`.

rule.statement: Every reply MUST stamp the internal tag (build / waitlist / decline / bug / clarify) before sending. Untagged tickets break reporting.
rule.rationale: Tagging-discipline practice — anchors Feedback Loop Customer Reply Templates rule `internal-tag-required`.

rule.statement: Decline replies MUST cite the relevant anti-roadmap entry by id. Bare 'we won't build this' replies invite re-litigation.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder-management research — anchors Feedback Loop Customer Reply Templates rule `decline-cites-anti-roadmap`.

rule.statement: Waitlist replies MUST link to a smoke page or signup form so demand is captured + visible. Pure email waitlists hide the signal.
rule.rationale: Demand-tracking research — anchors Feedback Loop Customer Reply Templates rule `waitlist-links-smoke-page`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
